Scenario.overview: Healthcare and Public Health Simple This attack involves exploiting exposed services, drive-by compromises, executing malicious payloads, scheduling tasks, and encrypting data to disrupt healthcare systems.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets File System Scheduled Task Malicious Payload
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: General Access Method Command and Scripting Interpreter Scheduled Task/Job Data Encrypted

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attackers exploit exposed RDP or SSH services to gain initial access.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attackers send malicious emails with attachments or links to employees' personal devices, leading to drive-by compromises.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: The attackers utilize PowerShell or batch scripts to deploy AvosLocker ransomware.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: The attackers schedule the execution of ransomware at system startup.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attackers encrypt sensitive data on healthcare systems.

Scenario.summary: This attack chain involves basic techniques to disrupt healthcare systems, including exploiting exposed services, drive-by compromises, executing malicious payloads, scheduling tasks, and encrypting data.
